title: Synthesis of goldene comprising single-atom layer gold
type1_summary: The Excel files are figure-ready source data for XRD/XPS plots, with labeled axes, sample-specific sheets, and fitted/processed spectral series. They are directly replot-ready.
type2_summary: No clear raw/unprocessed instrument-output file was identifiable from the provided inspection reports. The PDF could potentially contain supplementary raw or descriptive material, but its contents were not inspectable enough to classify as raw data.
classification_notes: The paper itself emphasizes raw microscopy and spectroscopy data, but the provided file inspection only exposed spreadsheet source data and one PDF. No microscopy image files or obvious raw instrument binaries were included in the inspection report. The PDF was conservatively treated as documentation because its contents were not available.

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper says supporting data are available in the article, Supplementary Information, and figshare. A PDF in the Nature source-data package is most likely supplementary documentation rather than a standalone data table.
file_classifications.file_evidence: PDF format, 4.7 MB, from nature_source_data. No tabular structure or machine-readable variables were provided in the inspection report.
file_classifications.reasoning: Without visible tabular columns or figure-specific numeric data, this file cannot be confidently treated as replot-ready data. It is most consistent with supplementary documentation or a PDF container for figures/text, not a direct dataset file.
file_classifications.ambiguity: High ambiguity because the PDF contents were not inspected; could contain supplementary figures or methods rather than data tables.

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper reports processed XRD/XPS-derived results and source data supporting figures. CMP progression is a process characterization dataset, and the file appears to contain plotted diffraction data for multiple samples/conditions.
file_classifications.file_evidence: Excel file with one sheet, 10 columns x 11916 rows. Columns are paired 2-theta and intensity series for Ti3SiC2, Ti3AuC2 before CMP, and Ti3AuC2 after 90 s / 270 s / 810 s CMP. This is organized as figure-ready line-scan data.
file_classifications.reasoning: The file is tabular, labeled by sample/condition, and directly replot-ready as XRD patterns with minimal processing. This matches Type 1 cleaned/source data.
file_classifications.key_columns_or_structure: 2 theta, Ti3SiC2, Ti3AuC2 before CMP, Ti3AuC2 after 90 s CMP, Ti3AuC2 after 270 s CMP, Ti3AuC2 after 810 s CMP

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper explicitly discusses XPS spectra, fitted peak components, and Au 4f binding-energy shifts. These are figure-supporting processed spectra rather than raw instrument exports in the publication context.
file_classifications.file_evidence: Excel file with 4 sheets: Reference Au, Ti3AuC2 etched by 0.2% Murakami, Ti3AuC2 etched by 0.5% Murakami, Pristine Ti3AuC2. Each sheet contains binding-energy axes and spectral channels, with curve fitting columns in the etched/pristine sheets.
file_classifications.reasoning: This is organized by sample and contains processed spectra plus fitting outputs, making it directly replot-ready and consistent with source data for Fig. 3. The presence of curve fitting indicates processed/cleaned data rather than raw detector output.
file_classifications.key_columns_or_structure: Binding Energy [eV], Au 4f, C 1s, O 1s, VB, Measurements, Curve fitting

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper's Fig. 3 is XPS comparison data with fitted components and extracted shifts, matching a figure-specific source-data deposit.
file_classifications.file_evidence: Excel file with the same 4 sheets and same structure as MOESM3: reference Au and three sample conditions, with binding-energy axes and curve-fitting columns.
file_classifications.reasoning: This is clearly figure-organized source data for XPS plots and fits. It can be directly plotted with minimal processing and is therefore Type 1.
file_classifications.key_columns_or_structure: Binding Energy [eV], Au 4f, C 1s, O 1s, VB, Measurements, Curve fitting

paper_analysis.summary: This paper reports a hydrofluoric-acid-free route to exfoliate single-atom-thick gold sheets (“goldene”) from Au-intercalated MAX-phase Ti3AuC2 by selective wet etching of Ti3C2 slabs using diluted Murakami’s reagent plus surfactants (CTAB or cysteine). The authors combine HR-STEM, XPS, and AIMD/DFT to show that the resulting Au monolayers are structurally stable, exhibit ~9% in-plane lattice contraction relative to bulk Au, and show an Au 4f binding-energy shift of 0.88 eV.
measurement_types: chemical mechanical polishing (process characterization, not a measurement)

figures.description: Schematic atomic positions and cross-sectional HR-STEM image of Ti3AuC2 along the [11̄20] orientation, showing Au, Ti, and C atomic columns.
Cross-sectional HR-STEM image after etching with Murakami’s reagent + CTAB, showing residual Ti3AuC2 on the left and goldene appearing at the etching front on the right.
Zoomed cross-sectional HR-STEM image of partly twisted goldene after removal of Ti3C2 slabs.
Schematic illustration of the preparation route for goldene from Ti3AuC2 by etching and surfactant stabilization.
Plan-view HR-STEM image of goldene sheets that pile up and entangle without coalescing into nanoparticles.
Plan-view HR-STEM image of goldene sheets resting on residual Ti3AuC2 / TiC seed layer / SiC substrate, showing ordered 1.8–1.9 nm spacing attributed to rippling.
HR-STEM image with STEM-EDS elemental mapping demonstrating Au as the constituent element of the nanosheets, with Ti3C2 MXene and Ti oxide residuals also visible.
XPS comparison of reference Au foil, pristine Ti3AuC2, and etched samples: Au 4f, Ti 2p, and C 1s spectra showing Au 4f shifts and disappearance of Ti–C / carbidic features after etching.
Plan and side orthographic views of a defect-free goldene monolayer supercell used in AIMD simulations at 300 K.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Raw experimental measurements include HR-STEM/HAADF-STEM images acquired at 60 keV and 300 keV, STEM-EDS elemental maps, and XPS spectra collected on Au foil, pristine Ti3AuC2, and etched samples. The paper also reports AIMD trajectories and DFT-calculated electronic/core-level properties as simulation outputs.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The authors state that the data supporting the findings are available within the article, the Supplementary Information, and a public figshare repository (https://doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.24584940).
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: The paper does not specify file formats for the repository. Raw data are implied by the experimental measurements described in Methods: HR-STEM/HAADF-STEM images, STEM-EDS maps, and XPS spectra. Instrument details are given (FEI Titan3 60–300 microscope; Kratos Axis Ultra DLD XPS), but no explicit raw-file naming convention or format is stated.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include fitted XPS peak decompositions, normalized spectra shown in figures, lattice-spacing estimates derived by length-scale normalization from Ti3AuC2 lattice parameters, and interpreted values such as the ~2.62 Å Au–Au spacing and 0.88 eV Au 4f shift. The paper also refers to supplementary sections for detailed analysis and additional etching-condition outcomes.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-ready data are clearly present in the article figures, including microscopy images, elemental maps, and fitted XPS spectra. The text does not explicitly say whether separate source-data files are provided per figure, but the figshare repository is stated to contain supporting data.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repositories are mentioned. Simulation software is named (VASP), but no scripts are provided or referenced.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is a mixed experimental + computational package. Experimental data are image/spectroscopy-heavy rather than tabular. Supplementary sections are important for additional microscopy, etching-condition comparisons, and AIMD details. The paper distinguishes raw measurements from interpreted quantities, but does not explicitly enumerate repository contents or file formats.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: Methods describe direct acquisition of HR-STEM, STEM-EDS, and XPS measurements; Data availability says supporting data are available in the article, Supplementary Information, and figshare.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: Figures present processed/fitted XPS spectra, normalized spectra, and derived lattice-spacing/binding-energy values; the paper explicitly discusses fitted peak components and extracted spacings.
data_availability_section_relevant: The data supporting the findings of this work are available within the article, the corresponding Supplementary Information and the public data repository figshare
All spectra were charge-referenced to the Fermi edge
With length scale normalization from well-defined Ti3AuC2 lattice parameter values, we obtain Au–Au spacing of 2.62 Å

inspection_reports.note: PDF file, cannot inspect data content
